macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. economic calendar packed with housing, consumer, inflation, and Fed-related data
items.summary: The latest trading calendar shows several high-impact U.S. releases and events, including Redbook sales, pending home sales, housing starts, crude oil inventory data, PPI, consumer confidence, and speeches by Fed officials Waller and others. These events are likely to shape expectations for growth, inflation, and interest rates over the next trading sessions.

items.title: University of Michigan says the U.S. economy continues to expand, helped by the AI boom
items.summary: A new University of Michigan economic outlook says U.S. real GDP expanded in the first quarter of 2026 and argues that the AI boom is supporting growth. The report adds to the macro narrative by suggesting that technology investment is helping offset softer parts of the economy.

items.title: Steve Rattner warns weak job market is becoming a major economic issue
items.summary: In a recent commentary, Steve Rattner highlighted weakening labor-market conditions as a source of consumer dissatisfaction and broader economic concern. He pointed to job losses in several blue-collar sectors and rising difficulty for recent college graduates to find work.

items.title: NGFS releases report on macroeconomic and financial impacts of extreme weather
items.summary: The Network for Greening the Financial System published a report examining how extreme weather events affect growth, markets, and the financial system. The findings are relevant for risk assets, insurers, lenders, and policymakers assessing climate-related economic shocks.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin falls below $77,000 as market pressure builds
items.summary: Bitcoin traded in a wide range around $76,000-$77,800 over the last 24 hours, slipping below $77,000 amid broader market weakness. Several market trackers also flagged heavy selling by short-term holders.

items.title: Strategy buys $2 billion in Bitcoin in a week
items.summary: MicroStrategy co-founder Michael Saylor's Strategy reportedly added another $2 billion of Bitcoin in a single week, reinforcing its aggressive accumulation strategy. The purchase came as BTC price remained under pressure.

items.title: Ripple mints 39.4 million RLUSD as XRP Ledger activity jumps
items.summary: Ripple minted 39.4 million RLUSD over 24 hours, with the XRP Ledger seeing a notable increase in activity. The move underscores ongoing growth in Ripple's stablecoin and network usage.

items.title: Senate Banking advances market structure work as stablecoin regulation moves forward
items.summary: U.S. Senate Banking developments pointed to continued momentum on crypto market structure and stablecoin oversight. Separately, NCUA reportedly proposed new stablecoin issuer guidance, adding to the regulatory focus on digital assets.

items.title: SEC reportedly plans major IPO overhaul amid stablecoin supply topping $300 billion
items.summary: Reports suggested the SEC is preparing one of its biggest IPO framework changes in two decades, while stablecoin supply has surpassed $300 billion. The developments highlight growing regulatory attention as crypto-linked capital markets expand.

items.title: Checker raises $8 million to build stablecoin infrastructure
items.summary: Stablecoin infrastructure startup Checker raised $8 million from Galaxy Ventures and other investors. The funding adds to a wave of investment in payments and settlement tooling for digital assets.

items.title: Japan’s ruling party backs AI-blockchain financial system plan
items.summary: Japan's LDP reportedly endorsed a plan combining AI and blockchain for financial services, signaling continued policy support for digital infrastructure innovation. The proposal is aimed at modernizing parts of the country’s financial system.

items.title: Hyperliquid token HYPE surges to highest level since October
items.summary: HYPE rose to its highest level since October, trading near $48 as renewed interest in Hyperliquid and decentralized trading platforms intensified. The move was accompanied by bullish commentary from market observers.

items.title: Dogecoin ETF interest spikes despite muted Musk commentary
items.summary: Dogecoin-related U.S. ETF activity reportedly jumped sharply, even as Elon Musk remained quiet on the asset. The increase suggests continued speculative demand around DOGE near the $0.10 level.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta hires four more OpenAI researchers, report says
items.summary: Meta has added four more OpenAI researchers to its AI talent push, according to a report citing a person familiar with the hires. The move underscores the ongoing race among major AI companies to recruit top model-building talent.

items.title: Google Cloud unveils Gemini 3.5, Gemini Omni, and new agentic AI tools at Google I/O 2026
items.summary: Google announced a broad slate of AI updates for Cloud customers, including Gemini 3.5 Flash, a new multimodal Gemini Omni model, and expanded agent-building tools such as Google Antigravity and Antigravity CLI. The company also highlighted a new AI Content Detection API aimed at identifying AI-generated media.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: PlayStation signals major shift away from day-and-date PC releases for single-player exclusives
items.summary: Sony’s PlayStation Studios leadership reportedly told staff that future story-driven single-player exclusives will not launch on PC at the same time as PlayStation consoles. The move marks a notable change in PlayStation’s PC strategy as the company also faces scrutiny over a PS Plus price increase in some regions.

items.title: Sony raises PlayStation Plus prices for new subscribers
items.summary: PlayStation Plus is reportedly getting more expensive for new members, with increases affecting one-month and three-month plans in select markets. The pricing move has sparked backlash as players weigh the value of the service against rising subscription costs.

items.title: Xbox launches Player Voice to collect community feedback
items.summary: Microsoft has introduced Player Voice, a new feedback hub designed to help Xbox users submit and track suggestions for games and platform features. The initiative is being framed as a more visible effort to show the company is listening to its community.

items.title: Xbox branding changes spark discussion as Microsoft leans into a unified XBOX identity
items.summary: Microsoft’s gaming division appears to be reworking its branding around a more unified 'Xbox' identity, prompting debate among fans about the company’s platform strategy. The move comes alongside broader efforts to highlight ecosystem features and player feedback.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 starts strongly, driving fresh momentum for Xbox’s 2026 lineup
items.summary: Recent gaming coverage says Forza Horizon 6 is off to a hot start, generating strong early interest and chart performance. The game is being discussed as one of Xbox’s key releases helping shape the console maker’s current slate.

items.title: Subnautica 2 launches to huge early sales and player counts
items.summary: Subnautica 2 has reportedly sold 2 million copies within 12 hours and reached more than 600,000 concurrent players at launch. The performance stands out as one of the biggest gaming success stories of the past day despite the game’s troubled development background.

items.title: Nintendo announces Pictonico, a new photo-to-mini-game mobile title
items.summary: Nintendo has announced Pictonico, a new mobile game that turns user photos into mini-games. The app is expected to release later this month and adds to Nintendo’s growing mobile efforts.

items.title: Nintendo Switch system update 20.1.1 rolls out ahead of Switch 2 launch
items.summary: Nintendo Switch users have a new system update available, with version 20.1.1 reportedly fixing an outstanding issue before the Switch 2 arrives. The update is a smaller but timely pre-launch maintenance release.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
